These are called dark and deep sayings, because they require careful consideration. The law of God was given with a specific instruction to teach it diligently to their children, so that the church may endure forever. Also, so that the providences of God, both in mercy and in judgment, might encourage them to conform to the will of God. Observing the works of God greatly strengthens our resolve to keep his commandments. Hypocrisy is a direct path to apostasy; those who do not set their hearts right will not remain steadfast with God. Many parents, through negligence and wickedness, become destructive to their children. But young people, though they are obligated to submit in all lawful matters, must not obey sinful commands or copy sinful examples.

Sin discourages people and takes away their strength. Forgetfulness of God's works causes disobedience to his laws. This narrative describes a struggle between God’s goodness and man’s wickedness. The Lord hears all our complaints and doubts, and is greatly displeased. Those who will not believe in the power of God’s mercy will feel the intensity of his anger. People cannot truly trust in God's salvation as their ultimate happiness if they cannot trust his providence along the way. For everyone who asks, seeks, and knocks through faith and prayer, these doors of heaven will be opened at any time; and our distrust of God greatly worsens our sins. He expressed his displeasure at their provocation not by denying what they desired sinfully, but by granting it to them. Lust is never satisfied. Those who indulge in lust will never abandon it. Hearts must be truly softened by the Lord’s mercies or broken by his judgments. Those who continue to sin must expect continued trouble. And the reason we live with so little comfort and purpose is because we do not live by faith. Under these rebukes they professed repentance, but it was insincere, as they were not consistent. In Israel's history we see a reflection of our own hearts and lives. God’s patience, warnings, and mercies emboldened them to harden their hearts against his word. And the history of kingdoms is similar. Judgments and mercies have been disregarded until the measure of their sins was full. Even privileged churches have declined from the commandments of God. Even true believers remember that for many years they abused the kindness of Providence. When they reach heaven, how will they admire the Lord’s patience and mercy in bringing them to his kingdom!

Let those who receive mercy from God not become emboldened to sin because of it, for the mercies they receive will hasten its punishment; yet let those who are under Divine rebukes for sin not be discouraged from repentance. The Holy One of Israel will do what is most for his own glory and what is most for their good. Their forgetting past blessings led them to limit God in the future. God caused his people to go forth like sheep, and guided them in the wilderness as a shepherd cares for his flock. Thus, the true Joshua, Jesus, brings his church out of the wilderness; but no earthly Canaan or worldly advantages should make us forget that the church is still in the wilderness while in this world, and that there remains a far more glorious rest for the people of God.

After the Israelites were settled in Canaan, their children were like their fathers. God gave them his testimonies, but they turned back. Presumptuous sins render even Israelites hateful to God’s holiness and expose them to his justice. Those whom the Lord abandons become easy prey to the destroyer. And sooner or later, God will shame his enemies. He established a good government over his people; a king after his own heart. The psalmist rightly concludes with this culminating instance of God's favor to Israel, for David was a type of Christ, the great and good Shepherd, who was humbled first and then exalted; and concerning whom it was foretold that he would be filled with the Spirit of wisdom and understanding. All his subjects may rely on the uprightness of his heart and the skillfulness of his hands; and there will be no end to the increase of his government and peace. Every trial of human nature so far confirms the testimony of Scripture, that the heart is deceitful above all things, desperately wicked, and nothing but being created anew by the Holy Ghost can cure any person’s ungodliness.
